reforms.description: The government has introduced various initiatives aimed at enhancing the employability of older workers and increasing female participation in the labor force. This includes training programs specifically for older workers in information technology, reforms to disability pensions for older workers, and a framework law on lifelong learning that provides tax reductions for enterprise-based training.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives aim to improve the skills and employability of the workforce, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The framework law on lifelong learning was enacted, and training programs have been introduced.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant but not structural changes to workforce training and support systems.
reforms.source_quote: The government intends to reform the law on disability pensions to provide assistance for older workers who are no longer as productive.

reforms.description: The budget for the central government in 2001 includes significant increases in spending on research and development, aiming to enhance innovation capacity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D funding is expected to boost innovation capacity, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The budget proposal includes significant increases in R&D spending.
reforms.importance_rationale: The increase in R&D funding is a significant change in budget allocation aimed at enhancing innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The budget for the central government contains significant rises in spending on infrastructure projects, notably road construction, on education, and on research and development.

reforms.description: Luxembourg passed a new law granting electronic signatures the same legal standing as handwritten ones, becoming the first EU country to do so. This law aims to enhance the legal framework for electronic commerce, particularly in the financial services sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the legal framework for electronic commerce, this reform is expected to facilitate business transactions and innovation in the digital economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The law has been passed and is in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major overhaul of the legal framework for electronic commerce.
reforms.source_quote: A new law giving an electronic signature the same legal standing as a hand-written one was passed in July, making Luxembourg the first EU country to pass such a law.
